neutral.$description: Fifth status-tone fill, for neutral/offline-style indicators (e.g. Avatar's offline status dot). gray.500 on bg.surface: 4.24:1 light / 3.12:1 dark, both pass WCAG 1.4.11 non-text contrast (3:1) — verified 2026-07-18, shared across all 4 themes since gray.500 uniquely satisfies both.

neutral-strong.$description: Solid neutral fill for hosting text (Badge's solid variant, neutral tone) — bg.neutral (gray.500) was only verified for the 3:1 non-text case and falls short of 4.5:1 text contrast with either white (4.24:1) or dark (3.9:1) text. gray.600 with white text gives 5.60:1, passing everywhere — shared across all 4 themes (verified 2026-07-18).

tertiary.$description: gray.500 (original) on bg.surface was 4.24:1, failing AA (needs 4.5:1) for its literal-text uses (Text color="tertiary", Divider label, Input placeholder). gray.600 gives 5.60:1 (verified 2026-07-18), still lighter/more muted than text.secondary (gray.700, 7.44:1).

on-danger.$description: Text on a solid bg.danger fill. White on red.600 = 6.12:1, passes AA (verified 2026-07-18).

on-info.$description: Text on a solid bg.info fill (Badge solid variant). White on blue.600 = 6.01:1, passes AA everywhere (verified 2026-07-18) — a single value works for both light and dark themes.

on-success.$description: Text on a solid bg.success fill (Badge solid variant). White fails badly here (green.600 = 3.30:1, green.500 = 2.48:1); gray.950 passes everywhere (5.02:1 light / 6.67:1 dark, verified 2026-07-18), matching the dark-text precedent set by text.on-brand's emerald-dark override.

on-warning.$description: Text on a solid bg.warning fill (Badge solid variant). White fails badly here (amber.600 = 3.07:1, amber.500 = 2.33:1); gray.950 passes everywhere (5.38:1 light / 7.11:1 dark, verified 2026-07-18).

on-neutral.$description: Text on a solid bg.neutral-strong fill (Badge solid variant). White on gray.600 = 5.60:1, passes everywhere — shared across all 4 themes (verified 2026-07-18).

success.$description: green.700 on bg.success-subtle (green.100) was 4.08:1, failing AA — green.800 gives 6.01:1 (verified 2026-07-18). Symptom of the HSL-derived color scales not being perceptually even across families (see the OKLCH re-derivation item in 01-vision-and-goals.md); darkening here is strictly safe since it only improves contrast against lighter surfaces.
